transcript.message: Hello, thank you for taking the time to speak with me today. My name is Rohan, and I'm a recruiter at FinSpark, a fintech company that's passionate about leveraging AI to drive innovation in the financial sector. Can you start by telling me a bit about your current role and what you've been working on lately?

I'm Vihaan Chatterjee, nice to speak with you, Rohan. Currently, I'm working as an HR Manager at Tata Consultancy Services, where I've been handling various HR-related tasks, including talent acquisition, employee engagement, and process improvements. Lately, I've been involved in implementing some HR tech solutions to streamline our recruitment processes.

It sounds like you've had a diverse range of experiences in your current role, but I'm actually looking for a Senior AI Engineer to join our team. Can you tell me a bit about your background in AI engineering, and what motivated you to explore this field?

I don't have a direct background in AI engineering, but I've been working on some AI-related projects in my current role at TCS. I've been experimenting with natural language processing using libraries like Hugging Face Transformers and Sentence Transformers, and I've also been exploring the use of embeddings and fine-tuning large language models for specific tasks.

It's great that you've been experimenting with AI-related projects, and I'm curious to know what sparked your interest in this field. What specifically drew you to the fintech industry, and do you think your current experience and skills could be applied to a role like this Senior AI Engineer position at FinSpark?

I think my interest in AI was sparked by the potential it has to drive innovation and solve complex problems. As for the fintech industry, I've always been fascinated by the intersection of technology and finance, and I believe my experience in handling large datasets and implementing process improvements could be valuable in a fintech setting. However, I do want to clarify that my experience is more on the HR side, and I'm not sure if it directly translates to a technical role like Senior AI Engineer.

It's completely understandable to have a non-traditional background, and we've had candidates from various fields successfully transition into technical roles before. Given your interest in the fintech industry and AI, I'm curious to know if you've had any experience with programming languages, specifically Python, which is a key skill required for this role.

I do have some experience with Python, although it's not my primary language. In my current role, I've worked on some HR tech projects that involved using Python for data analysis and automation tasks. I've also dabbled in libraries like Pandas and NumPy, and I've used them to work with large datasets. However, I wouldn't say I'm an expert in Python, and I'd need to brush up on my skills if I were to take on a role that requires heavy Python development.

It sounds like you have a good foundation in Python, and with some practice, you could definitely develop your skills further. One of the key aspects of this role is evaluating and optimizing AI models, which involves understanding metrics like NDCG, MRR, and MAP. Have you had any experience working with these types of evaluation frameworks or metrics in your previous projects?

I've worked with some metrics like precision, recall, and F1 score in my previous projects, but I don't have direct experience with NDCG, MRR, and MAP. However, I've been experimenting with some AI-related projects, and I've come across these metrics in my research. I'm familiar with the concept of evaluating model performance, but I wouldn't say I have hands-on experience with these specific metrics.

Vihaan, thank you so much for taking the time to speak with me today and sharing your experiences and interests with me. I appreciate your honesty about your background and the skills you've developed so far. While this role may not be the best fit for you at this time, I want to encourage you to continue exploring your interests in AI and fintech, and I wish you all the best in your future endeavors. I'll be in touch within the next few days to let you know the next steps, and I appreciate your consideration of FinSpark as a potential employer. Thank you again, and I wish you continued success!

Thank you, Rohan, for having me in today and for the opportunity to discuss the role at FinSpark. I appreciate your honesty about the position not being a good fit for me at this time, and I'm grateful for the encouragement to continue exploring my interests in AI and fintech. I wish FinSpark all the best in finding the right candidate for the role, and I look forward to hearing from you in the coming days. Thank you again for your time, and I wish you a great day.
